message.content:
Additive one-line edit per file, supporting a BUK refactor where the `buc` console module (Tools/buk/buc_command.sh) becomes a front-end on the `buym` renderer (Tools/buk/buym_yelp.sh). Every GATEWAY (CLI or workbench entry point) that sources buc_command.sh must now ALSO source buym_yelp.sh, so buc's output functions can reach the renderer at runtime. No explicit kindle is needed — buc's render path lazy-kindles via zbuym_sentinel.

RULE — apply to each file below:

1. CLASSIFY the file:
   - GATEWAY = an entry point: it either calls `buc_execute ...` (usually near the bottom), OR is a `*_workbench.sh` with a main/route function dispatched by a launcher. Gateways OWN dependency-sourcing.
   - MODULE = a file that defines `z<prefix>_kindle()` / `z<prefix>_sentinel()` and is SOURCED BY other files (not an entry point), with NO `buc_execute`.
   - If MODULE -> SKIP IT (do not edit). Its consuming gateway carries the dependency.

2. For a GATEWAY: find where the file sources its OTHER buk dependency modules (e.g. buv_validation.sh, burd_regime.sh, buz_zipper.sh, bure_regime.sh, etc.) — this is EITHER at file top-level (header) OR inside a `furnish`/`*_furnish` function. Add exactly ONE new line in that same block, adjacent to those other dep sources:
       source "${<DIRVAR>}/buym_yelp.sh"
   Use the SAME directory variable the file already uses on its existing `source "...buc_command.sh"` line — copy the ${...} prefix VERBATIM (it may be ${BURD_BUK_DIR}, ${BUW_SCRIPT_DIR}, ${ZBUQ_SCRIPT_DIR}, etc.). Match surrounding indentation.
   - CRITICAL: if the file uses a furnish pattern (buc_command.sh sourced at header, other deps sourced inside a furnish function), DO NOT add buym at the header — the CLI header must source ONLY buc_command.sh. Put buym with the other deps inside furnish.
   - If buym_yelp.sh is ALREADY sourced anywhere in the file, leave the file unchanged.

3. Additive ONLY: add one source line, change nothing else. Do not reorder, do not touch other lines. Do not run tests or git.

FILES (all under Tools/buk/):
- bux_cli.sh
- buq_cli.sh
- buq_qualify.sh   (likely a MODULE — classify carefully, skip if module)
- buv_validation.sh   (likely a MODULE — classify carefully, skip if module)
- bure_cli.sh
- burs_cli.sh
- buw_workbench.sh
- buut_cli.sh
- burc_cli.sh

REPORT BACK — one line per file, exactly one of:
  <file>: ADDED at <header|furnish> using <dirvar>
  <file>: SKIPPED (module)
  <file>: ALREADY PRESENT
Then stop. Do not run any tests or git commands.

- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the current diff and apply the fixes — equivalent to /code-review --fix.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

2	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
3	#
4	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
5	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
6	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
7	#
8	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9	#
10	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14	# limitations under the License.
15	#
16	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
17	#
18	# Bash Qualify Utility Library - Tabtarget structural qualification
19	
20	set -euo pipefail
21	
22	# Multiple inclusion guard
23	test -z "${ZBUQ_INCLUDED:-}" || return 0
24	ZBUQ_INCLUDED=1
25	
26	# Source the console utility library + moorings-layout names (launcher subdir)
27	ZBUQ_SCRIPT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
28	source "${ZBUQ_SCRIPT_DIR}/buc_command.sh"
29	source "${ZBUQ_SCRIPT_DIR}/bubc_constants.sh"
30	
31	######################################################################
32	# Tabtarget structural qualification
33	
34	buq_tabtargets() {
35	  local z_tt_dir="${1:-}"
36	  local z_project_root="${2:-}"
37	  test -n "${z_tt_dir}"       || buc_die "buq_tabtargets: tabtarget directory required"
38	  test -n "${z_project_root}" || buc_die "buq_tabtargets: project root required"
39	  test -d "${z_tt_dir}"       || buc_die "buq_tabtargets: directory not found: ${z_tt_dir}"
40	  shift 2
41	
42	  # Remaining arguments are glob patterns for exempt tabtargets
43	  local z_exemptions=("$@")
44	
45	  buc_step "Qualifying tabtarget structure in ${z_tt_dir}"
46	
47	  local z_fail_files=()
48	  local z_fail_reasons=()
49	  local z_count=0
50	  local z_exempt_count=0
51	
52	  # Prescribed tabtarget form (from buut_tabtarget.sh generator):
53	  #   Line 1:       #!/bin/bash
54	  #   Line 2:       export BURD_LAUNCHER=launcher.<id>_workbench.sh
55	  #   Lines 3..N-1: optional export BURD_*=* flag lines
56	  #   Last line:    exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"
57	  # BURD_LAUNCHER names the moorings launcher as a bare basename; z-launcher.sh
58	  # resolves it directly under the launcher dir. The exec line is a byte-
59	  # identical constant in every tabtarget.
60	  local z_prescribed_shebang='#!/bin/bash'
61	  local z_launcher_prefix='export BURD_LAUNCHER='
62	  local z_prescribed_exec='exec "${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}/z-launcher.sh" "${0##*/}" "${@}"'
63	
64	  local z_file=""
65	  for z_file in "${z_tt_dir}"/*.sh; do
66	    test -e "${z_file}" || continue
67	    z_count=$((z_count + 1))
68	
69	    local z_basename="${z_file##*/}"
70	
71	    # Check exemption patterns
72	    local z_is_exempt=0
73	    local z_exempt_pattern=""
74	    for z_exempt_pattern in "${z_exemptions[@]+"${z_exemptions[@]}"}"; do
75	      case "${z_basename}" in
76	        ${z_exempt_pattern}) z_is_exempt=1; break ;;
77	      esac
78	    done
79	    if test "${z_is_exempt}" = "1"; then
80	      z_exempt_count=$((z_exempt_count + 1))
81	      continue
82	    fi
83	
84	    # Load file lines (load-then-iterate per BCG)
85	    local z_lines=()
86	    local z_line=""
87	    while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
88	      z_lines+=("${z_line}")
89	    done < "${z_file}"
90	
91	    local z_num_lines=${#z_lines[@]}
92	
93	    # Must have at least 3 lines: shebang, BURD_LAUNCHER, exec
94	    test "${z_num_lines}" -ge 3 || {
95	      z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
96	      z_fail_reasons+=("too few lines: ${z_num_lines} (minimum 3)")
97	      continue
98	    }
99	
100	    # Line 1: prescribed shebang
101	    test "${z_lines[0]}" = "${z_prescribed_shebang}" || {
102	      z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
103	      z_fail_reasons+=("line 1: expected '${z_prescribed_shebang}', got '${z_lines[0]}'")
104	      continue
105	    }
106	
107	    # Line 2: export BURD_LAUNCHER=<basename> resolving to a moorings launcher.
108	    local z_launcher_line="${z_lines[1]}"
109	    case "${z_launcher_line}" in
110	      "${z_launcher_prefix}"launcher.*_workbench.sh) ;;
111	      *)
112	        z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
113	        z_fail_reasons+=("line 2: expected '${z_launcher_prefix}launcher.<id>_workbench.sh', got '${z_launcher_line}'")
114	        continue
115	        ;;
116	    esac
117	    local z_launcher_basename="${z_launcher_line#"${z_launcher_prefix}"}"
118	    local z_launcher_path="${BUBC_moorings_dir}/${BUBC_launchers_subdir}/${z_launcher_basename}"
119	    test -f "${z_project_root}/${z_launcher_path}" || {
120	      z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
121	      z_fail_reasons+=("launcher not found: ${z_launcher_path}")
122	      continue
123	    }
124	
125	    # Middle lines (3..N-1): must be export BURD_*=*
126	    local z_middle_ok=1
127	    local z_i=2
128	    local z_last_idx=$((z_num_lines - 1))
129	    while test "${z_i}" -lt "${z_last_idx}"; do
130	      case "${z_lines[$z_i]}" in
131	        'export BURD_'*'='*) ;;
132	        *)
133	          z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
134	          z_fail_reasons+=("line $((z_i + 1)): expected 'export BURD_*=*', got '${z_lines[$z_i]}'")
135	          z_middle_ok=0
136	          break
137	          ;;
138	      esac
139	      z_i=$((z_i + 1))
140	    done
141	    test "${z_middle_ok}" = "1" || continue
142	
143	    # Last line: the byte-identical constant exec through the z-launcher trampoline.
144	    test "${z_lines[$z_last_idx]}" = "${z_prescribed_exec}" || {
145	      z_fail_files+=("${z_basename}")
146	      z_fail_reasons+=("last line: expected '${z_prescribed_exec}', got '${z_lines[$z_last_idx]}'")
147	      continue
148	    }
149	  done
150	
151	  local z_checked=$((z_count - z_exempt_count))
152	  local z_summary="Checked ${z_checked} tabtargets"
153	  test "${z_exempt_count}" = "0" || z_summary="${z_summary} (${z_exempt_count} exempt)"
154	  buc_log_args "${z_summary}"
155	
156	  if (( ${#z_fail_files[@]} )); then
157	    local z_j=0
158	    for z_j in "${!z_fail_files[@]}"; do
159	      buc_warn "${z_fail_files[$z_j]}: ${z_fail_reasons[$z_j]}" || buc_die "Failed to warn"
160	    done
161	    buc_die "Tabtarget qualification failed: ${#z_fail_files[@]} of ${z_checked} tabtargets"
162	  fi
163	
164	  buc_log_args "All ${z_checked} tabtargets structurally valid"
165	}
166	
167	######################################################################
168	# Shellcheck qualification
169	
170	buq_shellcheck() {
171	  local z_tools_dir="${1:-${BURD_TOOLS_DIR:-}}"
172	  local z_rcfile="${2:-${BURD_BUK_DIR:+${BURD_BUK_DIR}/busc_shellcheckrc}}"
173	  local z_result_file="${3:-${BURD_TEMP_DIR:+${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/buq_shellcheck_results.txt}}"
174	  test -n "${z_tools_dir}"   || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: tools directory required"
175	  test -n "${z_rcfile}"      || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: rcfile path required"
176	  test -n "${z_result_file}" || buc_die "buq_shellcheck: result file path required"
177	
178	  buc_step "Running shellcheck qualification"
179	
180	  test -f "${z_rcfile}"    || buc_die "Shellcheck rcfile not found: ${z_rcfile}"
181	  test -d "${z_tools_dir}" || buc_die "Tools directory not found: ${z_tools_dir}"
182	
183	  command -v shellcheck >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "shellcheck not found — install from https://www.shellcheck.net"
184	
185	  # Collect .sh files (load-then-iterate per BCG)
186	  local z_files=()
187	  local z_file=""
188	  while IFS= read -r z_file || test -n "${z_file}"; do
189	    z_files+=("${z_file}")
190	  done < <(find "${z_tools_dir}" -name '*.sh' -type f | sort)
191	
192	  local -r z_file_count=${#z_files[@]}
193	  buc_log_args "Found ${z_file_count} shell files under ${z_tools_dir}"
194	
195	  test "${z_file_count}" -gt 0 || buc_die "No .sh files found under ${z_tools_dir}"
196	
197	  # Run shellcheck — capture output to temp file for forensics
198	  local z_status=0
199	  shellcheck --rcfile="${z_rcfile}" -S style -f gcc "${z_files[@]}" \
200	    > "${z_result_file}" 2>&1 \
201	    || z_status=$?
202	
203	  if test "${z_status}" = "0"; then
204	    buc_step "Shellcheck qualification passed: ${z_file_count} files clean"
205	    return 0
206	  fi
207	
208	  # Count and display findings
209	  local z_finding_count=0
210	  local z_discard
211	  while IFS= read -r z_discard || test -n "${z_discard:-}"; do
212	    z_finding_count=$((z_finding_count + 1))
213	  done < "${z_result_file}"
214	  buc_log_args "Shellcheck findings: ${z_finding_count} (see ${z_result_file})"
215	
216	  local z_line=""
217	  while IFS= read -r z_line || test -n "${z_line}"; do
218	    buc_warn "${z_line}"
219	  done < "${z_result_file}"
220	
221	  buc_die "Shellcheck qualification failed: ${z_finding_count} findings across ${z_file_count} files"
222	}
223	
224	# eof

326	# Internal check capture — validates a single enrolled variable by roll index.
327	# Returns error detail on stdout (empty = pass).
328	# Echo "${BUV_check_gated}" when gate doesn't match (caller decides skip behavior).
329	# Echo "${BUV_check_fail}<detail>" on validation failure.
330	zbuv_check_capture() {
331	  zbuv_sentinel
332	
333	  local z_idx="${1:-}"
334	  local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_idx]}"
335	  local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_idx]}"
336	  local z_gate_var="${z_buv_gate_var_roll[$z_idx]}"
337	  local z_gate_val="${z_buv_gate_val_roll[$z_idx]}"
338	  local z_p1="${z_buv_p1_roll[$z_idx]}"
339	  local z_p2="${z_buv_p2_roll[$z_idx]}"
340	
341	  # Gating check — if gated and gate doesn't match, skip (pass)
342	  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
343	    local z_gate_actual="${!z_gate_var:-}"
344	    if test "${z_gate_actual}" != "${z_gate_val}"; then
345	      echo "${BUV_check_gated}"
346	      return 0
347	    fi
348	  fi
349	
350	  # Unset detection — distinguish "not set" from "set but empty"
351	  if test -z "${!z_varname+x}"; then
352	    case "${z_type}" in
353	      string|secret|gname)
354	        if test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then return 0; fi ;;
355	      list_string|list_ipv4|list_gname|list_cidr|list_domain)
356	        return 0 ;;
357	    esac
358	    echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} is not set (missing from .env?)"
359	    return 0
360	  fi
361	
362	  local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
363	
364	  case "${z_type}" in
365	
366	    string|secret)
367	      if test "${z_p1}" = "0" && test -z "${z_val}"; then
368	        return 0
369	      fi
370	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
371	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
372	        return 0
373	      fi
374	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
375	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
376	        return 0
377	      fi
378	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
379	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
380	        return 0
381	      fi
382	      ;;
383	
384	    xname)
385	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
386	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
387	        return 0
388	      fi
389	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
390	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
391	        return 0
392	      fi
393	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
394	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
395	        return 0
396	      fi
397	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$ ]] || {
398	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must start with letter and contain only letters, numbers, underscore, hyphen, got '${z_val}'"
399	        return 0
400	      }
401	      ;;
402	
403	    gname)
404	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
405	        if test "${z_p1}" -gt 0; then
406	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
407	          return 0
408	        fi
409	        return 0
410	      fi
411	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
412	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
413	        return 0
414	      fi
415	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
416	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
417	        return 0
418	      fi
419	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ ]] || {
420	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must match ^[a-z][a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9]$ (lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; start with a letter; end with letter/digit), got '${z_val}'"
421	        return 0
422	      }
423	      ;;
424	
425	    fqin)
426	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
427	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
428	        return 0
429	      fi
430	      if test "${#z_val}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
431	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
432	        return 0
433	      fi
434	      if test "${#z_val}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
435	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_val}' (${#z_val})"
436	        return 0
437	      fi
438	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9:._/@-]*$ ]] || {
439	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must start with letter/number and contain only letters, numbers, colons, dots, underscores, hyphens, forward slashes, at-signs, got '${z_val}'"
440	        return 0
441	      }
442	      ;;
443	
444	    bool)
445	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
446	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
447	        return 0
448	      fi
449	      if test "${z_val}" != "0" && test "${z_val}" != "1"; then
450	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be 0 or 1, got: '${z_val}'"
451	        return 0
452	      fi
453	      ;;
454	
455	    enum)
456	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
457	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
458	        return 0
459	      fi
460	      local z_choice
461	      local z_found=0
462	      for z_choice in ${z_p1}; do
463	        if test "${z_val}" = "${z_choice}"; then
464	          z_found=1
465	          break
466	        fi
467	      done
468	      if test "${z_found}" = "0"; then
469	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must be one of: ${z_p1}, got '${z_val}'"
470	        return 0
471	      fi
472	      ;;
473	
474	    decimal)
475	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
476	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
477	        return 0
478	      fi
479	      if test "${z_val}" -ge "${z_p1}" && test "${z_val}" -le "${z_p2}"; then
480	        return 0
481	      fi
482	      echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} value '${z_val}' must be between ${z_p1} and ${z_p2}"
483	      return 0
484	      ;;
485	
486	    odref)
487	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
488	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
489	        return 0
490	      fi
491	      local z_re='^[a-z0-9.-]+(:[0-9]{2,5})?/([a-z0-9._-]+/)*[a-z0-9._-]+@sha256:[0-9a-f]{64}$'
492	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ${z_re} ]] || {
493	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} has invalid image reference format (require host[:port]/repo@sha256:<64hex>), got '${z_val}'"
494	        return 0
495	      }
496	      ;;
497	
498	    ipv4)
499	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
500	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
501	        return 0
502	      fi
503	      [[ "${z_val}" =~ ^([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$ ]] || {
504	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} has invalid IPv4 format: '${z_val}'"
505	        return 0
506	      }
507	      ;;
508	
509	    port)
510	      if test -z "${z_val}"; then
511	        echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} must not be empty"
512	        return 0
513	      fi
514	      if test "${z_val}" -ge 1 && test "${z_val}" -le 65535; then
515	        return 0
516	      fi
517	      echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} value '${z_val}' must be between 1 and 65535"
518	      return 0
519	      ;;
520	
521	    list_string)
522	      local z_item
523	      local z_item_num=0
524	      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
525	        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
526	        if test "${#z_item}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then
527	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be at least ${z_p1} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
528	          return 0
529	        fi
530	        if test "${#z_item}" -gt "${z_p2}"; then
531	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} must be no more than ${z_p2} chars, got '${z_item}' (${#z_item})"
532	          return 0
533	        fi
534	      done
535	      ;;
536	
537	    list_ipv4)
538	      local z_item
539	      local z_item_num=0
540	      for z_item in ${z_val}; do
541	        z_item_num=$((z_item_num + 1))
542	        [[ "${z_item}" =~ ^([0-9]{1,3}\.){3}[0-9]{1,3}$ ]] || {
543	          echo "${BUV_check_fail}${z_varname} item #${z_item_num} has invalid IPv4 format: '${z_item}'"

545	        }
546	      done

548

554	        if test "${#z_item}" -lt "${z_p1}"; then

610	# buv_scope_sentinel SCOPE PREFIX — die if any PREFIX_ vars exist that are not enrolled in SCOPE
611	# Usage: buv_scope_sentinel RBRN RBRN_
612	buv_scope_sentinel() {
613	  zbuv_sentinel
614	
615	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
616	  local z_prefix="${2:-}"
617	  test -n "${z_scope}"  || buc_die "buv_scope_sentinel: scope required"
618	  test -n "${z_prefix}" || buc_die "buv_scope_sentinel: prefix required"
619	
620	  # Build lookup string from enrolled varnames for this scope
621	  local z_known=" "
622	  local z_i
623	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
624	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
625	    z_known="${z_known}${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]} "
626	  done
627	
628	  # Scan environment for unexpected vars with this prefix
629	  local z_unexpected=()
630	  local z_var
631	  for z_var in $(compgen -v "${z_prefix}"); do
632	    case "${z_known}" in
633	      *" ${z_var} "*) : ;;
634	      *) z_unexpected+=("${z_var}") ;;
635	    esac
636	  done
637	
638	  if test "${#z_unexpected[@]}" -gt 0; then
639	    buc_die "Unexpected ${z_prefix}* variables not enrolled in ${z_scope}: ${z_unexpected[*]}"
640	  fi
641	}
642	
643	# buv_docker_env SCOPE ARRAY_VAR — populate ARRAY_VAR with -e VARNAME=val pairs for all enrolled vars in SCOPE
644	# Usage: buv_docker_env RBRN ZRBRN_DOCKER_ENV
645	buv_docker_env() {
646	  zbuv_sentinel
647	
648	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
649	  local z_array_var="${2:-}"
650	  test -n "${z_scope}"     || buc_die "buv_docker_env: scope required"
651	  test -n "${z_array_var}" || buc_die "buv_docker_env: array variable name required"
652	  [[ "${z_array_var}" =~ ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ ]] \
653	    || buc_die "buv_docker_env: invalid array variable name: '${z_array_var}'"
654	
655	  eval "${z_array_var}=()"
656	
657	  local z_i
658	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
659	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
660	    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
661	    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
662	    eval "${z_array_var}+=(\"-e\" \"${z_varname}=${z_val}\")"
663	  done
664	}
665	
666	# buv_vet SCOPE — iterate all enrolled vars in scope; die on first failure
667	buv_vet() {
668	  zbuv_sentinel
669	
670	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
671	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_vet: scope required"
672	
673	  local z_i
674	  local z_err
675	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
676	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
677	    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
678	    test -z "${z_err}" || test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}" || buc_die "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
679	  done
680	}
681	
682	# buv_lock SCOPE — make all enrolled variables in scope readonly
683	# Call after enforce succeeds. Prevents downstream mutation of validated config.
684	buv_lock() {
685	  zbuv_sentinel
686	
687	  local -r z_scope="${1:-}"
688	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_lock: scope required"
689	
690	  local z_i
691	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
692	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
693	    readonly "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
694	  done
695	}
696	
697	buv_export_and_lock() {
698	  zbuv_sentinel
699	
700	  local -r z_scope="${1:-}"
701	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_export_and_lock: scope required"
702	
703	  local z_i
704	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
705	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
706	    export "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
707	    readonly "${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
708	  done
709	}
710	
711	# buv_report SCOPE "Label" — rich per-variable display; returns non-zero if any failed
712	buv_report() {
713	  zbuv_sentinel
714	
715	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
716	  local z_label="${2:-}"
717	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_report: scope required"
718	  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_report: label required"
719	
720	  local z_any_failed=0
721	
722	  buc_step "${z_label}"
723	
724	  local z_i
725	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
726	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
727	
728	    local z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
729	    local z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
730	    local z_val="${!z_varname:-}"
731	    local z_err
732	
733	    # Secret redaction — replace display value before PASS/FAIL output
734	    local z_display_val="${z_val}"
735	    if test "${z_type}" = "secret" && test -n "${z_val}"; then
736	      z_display_val="(redacted — ${#z_val} chars)"
737	    fi
738	
739	    z_err=$(zbuv_check_capture "${z_i}")
740	    if test -z "${z_err}"; then
741	      buc_step "  PASS  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]"
742	    elif test "${z_err}" = "${BUV_check_gated}"; then
743	      buc_step "  SKIP  ${z_varname} (gated)"
744	    else
745	      buc_step "  FAIL  ${z_varname}=${z_display_val} [${z_type}]: ${z_err#"${BUV_check_fail}"}"
746	      z_any_failed=1
747	    fi
748	  done
749	
750	  return "${z_any_failed}"
751	}
752	
753	# zbuv_group_gate_recite SCOPE TITLE — look up group gate from registry
754	# Sets ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR and ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL (empty if ungated).
755	zbuv_group_gate_recite() {
756	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
757	  local z_title="${2:-}"
758	
759	  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR=""
760	  ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL=""
761	
762	  local z_s
763	  for z_s in "${!z_buv_grp_scope_roll[@]}"; do
764	    if test "${z_buv_grp_scope_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_scope}" \
765	      && test "${z_buv_grp_title_roll[$z_s]}" = "${z_title}"; then
766	      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR="${z_buv_grp_gate_var_roll[$z_s]}"
767	      ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL="${z_buv_grp_gate_val_roll[$z_s]}"
768	      return 0
769	    fi
770	  done
771	}
772	
773	# zbuv_req_status INDEX — derive req/opt/cond from enrollment data
774	# Sets ZBUV_REQ_STATUS.
775	zbuv_req_status() {
776	  local z_idx="${1:-}"
777	  local z_gate_var="${z_buv_gate_var_roll[$z_idx]}"
778	  local z_p1="${z_buv_p1_roll[$z_idx]}"
779	
780	  if test -n "${z_gate_var}"; then
781	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="cond"
782	  elif test "${z_p1}" = "0"; then
783	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="opt"
784	  else
785	    ZBUV_REQ_STATUS="req"
786	  fi
787	}
788	
789	# buv_render SCOPE "Label" [FILE_PATH] — render all enrolled vars via bupr_ presentation
790	# Walks enrollment rolls grouped by group, applying group-level gates.
791	# Optional FILE_PATH displays a gray "File: <path>" line under the title; empty/omitted skips it.
792	# Requires bupr (PresentationRegime) to be kindled.
793	buv_render() {
794	  zbuv_sentinel
795	  zbupr_sentinel
796	
797	  local z_scope="${1:-}"
798	  local z_label="${2:-}"
799	  local z_file_path="${3:-}"
800	  test -n "${z_scope}" || buc_die "buv_render: scope required"
801	  test -n "${z_label}" || buc_die "buv_render: label required"
802	
803	  local z_current_group=""
804	  local z_i
805	  local z_group=""
806	  local z_varname=""
807	  local z_type=""
808	  local z_desc=""
809	
810	  echo ""
811	  echo "${BUC_white}${z_label}${BUC_reset}"
812	  if test -n "${z_file_path}"; then
813	    echo "  ${BUC_gray}File: ${z_file_path}${BUC_reset}"
814	  fi
815	  echo ""
816	
817	  for z_i in "${!z_buv_scope_roll[@]}"; do
818	    test "${z_buv_scope_roll[$z_i]}" = "${z_scope}" || continue
819	
820	    z_group="${z_buv_group_roll[$z_i]}"
821	    z_varname="${z_buv_varname_roll[$z_i]}"
822	    z_type="${z_buv_type_roll[$z_i]}"
823	    z_desc="${z_buv_desc_roll[$z_i]}"
824	
825	    # Group transition — close previous, open new
826	    if test "${z_group}" != "${z_current_group}"; then
827	      if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
828	        bupr_section_end
829	      fi
830	      z_current_group="${z_group}"
831	
832	      zbuv_group_gate_recite "${z_scope}" "${z_group}"
833	      if test -n "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}"; then
834	        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAR}" "${ZBUV_GRP_GATE_VAL}"
835	      else
836	        bupr_section_begin "${z_group}"
837	      fi
838	    fi
839	
840	    zbuv_req_status "${z_i}"
841	    bupr_section_item "${z_varname}" "${z_type}" "${ZBUV_REQ_STATUS}" "${z_desc}"
842	  done
843	
844	  # Close final group
845	  if test -n "${z_current_group}"; then
846	    bupr_section_end
847	  fi
848	}
849	
850	# eof
851
